title: Semantik Architect Frame Schema — Relational: Temporal Relation
description: JSON shape for the temporal relation frame, corresponding to the temporal relation family in the frame inventory and the TemporalRelationFrame semantic type. This frame captures ordering, anchoring, duration, and recurrence relations between events, states, entities standing for eventive states, or explicit time spans (e.g. 'X happened before Y', 'X has been ongoing since 2010', 'X takes place every July').

frame_type.description: Canonical frame family identifier. `relation.temporal` is the preferred value. Short/legacy aliases are accepted for compatibility with existing routing and older payloads.

id.description: Optional stable identifier for this relation instance (e.g. statement cluster ID, row ID, or source key).

left.description: Left-side argument of the temporal relation. This may be an event, an entity standing for an implicit event/state (e.g. 'the war', 'the treaty', 'the presidency'), or an explicit temporal anchor / span.

right.description: Right-side argument of the temporal relation. This may be an event, an entity standing for an implicit event/state, or an explicit temporal anchor / span.

relation.description: Temporal relation label between `left` and `right`. Typical values include `before`, `after`, `since`, `until`, `during`, `throughout`, `overlapping`, `around`, `by`, and `contemporary_with`. The inventory remains intentionally open; downstream logic may normalize these into a smaller canonical set.

left_time.description: Optional explicit time span associated with the left argument, when `left` itself is an entity/event without a populated time field or when a normalized anchor should be attached separately.

right_time.description: Optional explicit time span associated with the right argument, when `right` itself does not fully encode the needed temporal anchor.

recurrence.description: Optional recurrence label for seasonal or periodic relations, such as `annual`, `monthly`, `weekly`, `every_July`, or `every_spring`.

certainty.description: Optional confidence score for the relation in the range [0, 1]. `1.0` means fully trusted / asserted.

source_id.description: Optional identifier for the source statement, record, or provenance bundle from which this relation was derived.

attributes.description: Optional structured attribute bag for temporal details that do not yet warrant dedicated top-level fields, such as calendar system, granularity, seasonality notes, or normalization hints.

extra.description: Opaque metadata preserved for traceability, debugging, provenance, or source-system round-tripping. This should not affect generic realization directly.

EntityRef.description: Lightweight reference to a discourse entity compatible with the project’s core semantic Entity shape.

id.description: Stable identifier for the entity, e.g. a Wikidata QID, local database ID, or URI.

qid.description: Optional explicit Wikidata-style identifier kept for compatibility with existing semantic objects and legacy payloads.

name.description: Preferred surface label or canonical display name.

gender.description: Coarse gender category for human entities (e.g. `female`, `male`, `nonbinary`, `unknown`).

human.description: Whether this entity denotes a human.

entity_type.description: Optional coarse type hint such as `person`, `organization`, `place`, `event`, or `concept`.

lemmas.description: Optional language-neutral lexical hints associated with the entity.

features.description: Arbitrary feature dictionary for downstream morphology or construction logic.

extra.description: Arbitrary metadata such as source-system fields, aliases, or raw normalization traces.

LocationRef.description: Reference to a location compatible with the project’s core Location shape.

kind.description: Optional coarse location kind such as `city`, `country`, `region`, or `venue`.

features.description: Optional feature dictionary for preposition choice or geographic subtyping.

TimeSpan.description: Coarse time span or single date compatible with the project’s core TimeSpan shape. Use this for explicit temporal anchors, date-like points, and rough durations.

start_year.description: Starting year, or the main year of interest for a point-like anchor.

end_year.description: Optional end year for a range.

approximate.description: Whether the time span is approximate or inferred.

extra.description: Arbitrary metadata such as full ISO strings, calendar system, original source strings, or normalization details.

EventRef.description: Lightweight reference to a semantic event or state compatible with the project’s core Event shape.

event_type.description: Free-form event or state type label, such as `war`, `treaty`, `project`, `festival`, or `presidency`.

time.description: When the event took place or holds.

location.description: Where the event took place, if location is relevant and known.

participants.description: Optional role-to-participant mapping. Values may be a single entity or an array of entities.

properties.description: Optional structured event properties preserved from semantic normalization.

meta.description: Optional event metadata bag for project-specific semantic annotations.

extra.description: Arbitrary event metadata and source-system fields.
